meta.strategy_type: regime-gated, vol-targeted long-or-flat volatility overlay on daily BTC OHLCV (OHLCV-only)
meta.position_bounds: long-or-flat [0, 1] — max_leverage=1.0, never levered or short
meta.regimes: 4 genuine volatility regimes (GaussianHMM K=4, filtered/causal, TRAIN-only); no degenerate states
meta.derivatives_note: funding/open-interest/long-short are unavailable on CMC and are excluded (D2)
meta.verdict: NO EDGE in this configuration: the overlay reduces drawdown and realized vol on all of TRAIN/VAL/TEST, but does NOT beat buy-and-hold on Sharpe or return on any segment. Reported, not tuned.
meta.finding: In crypto, BTC's positive drift is concentrated in HIGH-volatility regimes (bull runs are volatile), so de-risking high vol misses the upside — opposite of the equities intuition. The regime gate is correctly signed for its risk-reduction design; flipping it to chase the in-sample upside would be overfitting, so it was NOT changed.
meta.disclaimer: Backtest on REAL historical daily BTC OHLCV; regime discipline + risk transform only; NO claim of live edge or alpha. Every metric is TRAIN/VAL/TEST-labeled; compare the overlay against the buy-and-hold benchmark on the same basis.

obs_standardization.note: fit on TRAIN obs only; applied to TRAIN/VAL/TEST alike (affine, no lookahead). State means below are reported in original units (inverse-transformed).

signal.note: Derivatives unavailable on CMC (D2) → no positioning/funding signal. Baseline exposure = long BTC beta, flattened by the regime gate in de-risked/turbulent states. This is NOT an alpha claim; see the buy-and-hold benchmark.

sizing.target_vol_source: TRAIN median of HAR forecast vol

guards.lookahead: none — embargo=30 bars, all scalers/thresholds/HAR/HMM fit on TRAIN only, filtered HMM inference

rationale.plain_summary: This strategy was built to REDUCE RISK, not chase profit: on real daily BTC data it reduces drawdown and volatility across all segments (TRAIN/VAL/TEST), but does not beat buy-and-hold on Sharpe or return.
rationale.what_it_does: Detects 4 daily BTC volatility regimes with a Gaussian HMM (filtered/causal, fit on TRAIN only), then holds long-or-flat positions: full size in calm/low-vol regimes, reduced in high-vol, and flat in turbulent — vol-target sized and clipped to [0, 1] (never levered or short).
rationale.honest_verdict: No directional edge in this configuration — purely a risk overlay; results reported as-is and NOT tuned.
rationale.key_finding: BTC's upside is concentrated in HIGH-volatility regimes (bull runs are volatile), so de-risking the high-vol regime also misses the gains — the opposite of the equities intuition.

decisions.why: The CMC submission path is locked to daily bars (D1: bars_per_year=365); all RV/HAR windows and horizons are computed per day.
CMC does not provide directional signals (funding / open-interest / long-short ratio), so the daily CMC path is purely OHLCV (D2).
Without a directional signal, base exposure = long BTC beta, then flattened by the regime gate in high-vol/turbulent states; clipped to [0, 1] so never levered or short.

decisions.choice: Did not flip the regime gate
decisions.why: Flipping the gate to chase the in-sample visible upside would be overfitting / return-chasing, so it was deliberately left unchanged.

candidates_evaluated.why: CMC F&G history only starts 2023-06-29, while TRAIN ends 2022-09-18 — zero overlap with TRAIN, so TRAIN IC = NaN and cannot be validated without lookahead; rejected before full fetch.
Unavailable on CMC (D2) — no historical endpoint exists, so not used and not claimed.
Pure derivative of OHLCV prices — no new information beyond what HMM already uses, so it adds nothing to the 'CMC data' story.

limitations: Low regime persistence (average ~1.5 bars per run, switch rate ~66%) means this behaves more like a fast volatility classifier than a long-horizon regime detector in practice; documented as future work and deliberately NOT 'fixed' to avoid overfitting to this sample.
